seoTitle: How to Delete a Story in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to permanently delete a story issue in Jira, including what to check first since the action can't be undone.
intro: Sometimes a story gets created by mistake or turns out to be a duplicate, and the cleanest fix is to delete it outright. This guide covers how to remove a story issue in Jira and what to verify before doing so.

steps.heading: Confirm the story should be deleted, not just closed
steps.body: If the story has any history or value as a record, consider closing or marking it as won't-do instead, since deletion is permanent.

steps.heading: Open the story
steps.body: Navigate to the specific story issue you want to remove.

steps.heading: Check for linked work
steps.body: Look for any linked issues, subtasks, or comments that might be affected by removing the story.

steps.heading: Use the delete option
steps.body: Select the delete action from the issue's menu to remove the story from your project.

steps.heading: Confirm the deletion
steps.body: Jira will ask you to confirm, since this action is permanent and cannot be undone once completed.

faq.question: Can I recover a deleted story in Jira?
faq.answer: No, deleting an issue in Jira is permanent, so it's worth double-checking before confirming the action.

faq.question: What happens to subtasks when I delete a story?
faq.answer: Subtasks attached to a deleted story are typically deleted along with it, so review any subtasks before proceeding.

faq.question: Should I delete or close an outdated story?
faq.answer: If you want to preserve a record for reporting or history purposes, closing or marking it won't-do is usually the safer choice over outright deletion.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to delete a story?
faq.answer: Yes, deleting issues typically requires delete permissions on the project, which may be restricted depending on your role.
